summary: Account Deletion (Delete Account Button) bug fix verification completed. All 10 backend tests PASSED and 11 frontend UI tests PASSED. The fix for missing Prisma onDelete cascade rules is working correctly - users with lead data can now be deleted without foreign key constraint errors.

issues: Minor chart width warning in console (non-blocking): 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'

Route Audit: GET /api/settings with auth returns 200

Route Audit: GET /api/sequences/dashboard with auth returns 200
DELETE /api/user/account with wrong password returns 401 'Invalid password'

After deletion, login fails with 401 (account no longer exists)

Frontend: Cancel button present and works (hides confirmation form)

Frontend: All data-testid attributes present for testing

seed_data_creation: Created disposable test users with leads for cascade deletion testing (auto-cleanup via deletion)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Account deletion bug fix is verified. The original issue was missing onDelete cascade rules in Prisma schema causing foreign key constraint errors when deleting users with existing data. Fix applied to 5 User relations: Activity.user (SetNull), Booking.createdBy (Cascade), Lead.assignedTo (Cascade), Message.sender (SetNull), Quote.createdBy (Cascade). All cascade deletions now work correctly. Frontend flow: Settings gear -> Account tab -> Delete My Account -> Enter password -> Delete Forever. All data-testid attributes present for testing.

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: Delete Account button fails with 'Failed to delete account' error
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: 5 Prisma model relations to User were missing onDelete: Cascade/SetNull, causing foreign key constraint errors
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Added cascade rules to Lead.assignedTo, Quote.createdBy, Booking.createdBy (Cascade) and Activity.user, Message.sender (SetNull)
bug_fix_verification.verification_method: Created user with lead data, then successfully deleted account via API
